moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 10% to manage single name concentration.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Buy XLV at 5% to diversify into a high-quality sector.
This plan aims for capital preservation with a large cash buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% monthly returns, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. However, potential upside is limited compared to more aggressive strategies.

moves.note: Blend broad index and quality tech

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and reduce SPAL to 3% to keep leveraged ETFs small.
Trim SMH to 10% given its downtrend status.
Increase QQQI to 20% to balance income and growth.
Buy LRCX at 5% to add a high-scoring semiconductor name.
This plan maintains a 10% cash buffer, balancing risk and growth.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% monthly returns, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index and quality tech. It aims for moderate growth with controlled drawdown.

moves.note: Press what is working, strong P&L

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single name mandate.
Maintain SOXL at 8% as part of the leveraged ETF allocation.
Reduce SMH to 10% due to its downtrend status.
Increase CRWD to 15% to press a strong performer.
Buy LRCX at 10% and ASML at 7% to add high-scoring semiconductor setups.
This plan uses a smaller cash buffer and leans into leverage.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% monthly returns, aiming for stretch growth. It utilizes leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring setups, which significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential. Be aware that leveraged ETFs decay daily and this strategy can incur substantial losses quickly if stops are not strictly adhered to.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Rotate into income ETFs for yield

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and support income focus.
Sell all SOXL, SPAL, CRWD, and SMH to eliminate leverage and high-beta exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to rotate into income ETFs for steady distributions.
Reduce QQQ to 15% to maintain some broad index exposure.
This plan prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
A significant cash buffer will be maintained.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% monthly distributions, aiming to park cash for yield. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta names, prioritizing low drawdown. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
